Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely coexpressed with GAUT1 in most tissues and developmental stages, including primary- and secondary-wall-forming tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Anchors GAUT1 in the Golgi and supports HG biosynthesis as part of the GAUT1:GAUT7 complex
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reciprocal coimmunoprecipitation; BiFC; protein processing/localization analyses
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAUT7 is a proven component of the GAUT1:GAUT7 HG:GalAT complex. 2. It serves as the Golgi membrane anchor for posttranslationally cleaved GAUT1. 3. Both covalent and noncovalent interactions contribute to complex formation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential indirect lever on HG synthesis, but likely essential and pleiotropic
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Did not show HG:GalAT activity on its own upon heterologous expression; considered the membrane anchor rather than the catalytic HG:GalAT subunit.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal pectin arabinan content; putatively promotes RG-I arabinan biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant characterization; RG-I structural analysis; immunohistochemistry; BiFC/FRET dimerization assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. arad1 mutants have strongly reduced arabinan and 5-linked Araf in RG-I. 2. ARAD1 is implicated as a putative RG-I arabinan:α-1,5-arabinosyltransferase. 3. ARAD1 forms homo- and heterodimers with ARAD2.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate to alter arabinan-rich pectin architecture with relatively mild visible phenotypes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Biochemical activity had not yet been conclusively demonstrated; arad mutants showed no apparent growth phenotype.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to pectin arabinan biosynthesis; nonredundant with ARAD1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant characterization; immunohistochemistry; overexpression noncomplementation; BiFC/FRET dimerization assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARAD2 is implicated as a putative RG-I arabinan biosynthetic enzyme. 2. It is closely related to but nonredundant with ARAD1. 3. ARAD2 forms homo- and heterodimers with ARAD1.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for fine-tuning RG-I arabinan structure
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Biochemical enzyme activity not conclusively demonstrated in the review.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Putative SAM-dependent methyltransferase; QUA2 family; PF03141
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Putatively affects HG methylation and/or HG amount; mutants show reduced wall GalA/HG and adhesion defects
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype and wall composition analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QUA2/TSD2 is proposed as a putative HG methyltransferase. 2. qua2/tsd2 mutants are dwarf and defective in cell-cell adhesion. 3. Mutants show reduced wall GalA/HG, but a normal reported degree/distribution of pectin methylesterification complicates assignment.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential route to alter pectin amount/adhesion, but functional uncertainty and growth defects are major cautions
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarf growth and cell adhesion defects
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function as HG:MT remained unconfirmed and partly contradicted by phenotype data.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in tobacco BY-2; RNAi downregulation in Arabidopsis suspension cells; antibody labeling; proteomics association with GAUT1:GAUT7 complex
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QUA3 is implicated as a putative HG methyltransferase. 2. Overexpression increases radiolabeled methyl incorporation into HG-containing material. 3. RNAi causes increased low-esterified HG labeling, but some antibody data are inconsistent with a simple HG:MT assignment.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to adjust HG esterification and wall mechanics, pending definitive biochemical proof
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a putative interacting protein of the GAUT1:GAUT7 complex.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout and overexpression analyses; immunolabeling with HG esterification-sensitive antibodies
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CGR3 is a putative HG methyltransferase distinct from QUA2/QUA3 family members. 2. cgr3 knockout reduces highly esterified HG epitopes, while overexpression increases them. 3. Data support, but do not yet biochemically prove, an HG:MT role.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering pectin esterification state and wall properties
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional identity as HG:MT awaited biochemical confirmation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Putatively required for normal HG biosynthesis and influences xylan synthase activity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant wall analyses; microsomal enzyme activity assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAUT8/QUA1 is proposed to function in HG biosynthesis based on reduced HG/cell adhesion phenotypes. 2. qua1 mutants also show reduced xylan synthase activity, linking pectin and xylan biosynthesis. 3. Severe dwarf growth supports an important structural role.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for studying pectin–xylan coupling, but poor direct engineering target because of strong developmental defects
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact enzymatic activity had not been proven; may participate in both pectin-related and xylan-related wall architecture.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Xylan reducing-end structure and/or specific pectic domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal wall xylan deposition and affects pectin-associated GalA fractions
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant wall fractionation and structural analysis; enzyme activity comparisons in stem microsomes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. irx8/gaut12 mutants have reduced wall xylan and collapsed xylem despite normal/elevated xylan synthase activity. 2. Mutants also lose a small GalA-containing wall fraction, linking GAUT12 to pectin-related structure. 3. GAUT12 may synthesize a pectic or glycosyl substructure needed for xylan deposition.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highly relevant for biomass and secondary wall engineering, but manipulation may compromise vascular integrity
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Collapsed xylem, severe dwarf/sterility-related defects reported for mutants
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific enzymatic activity had not been demonstrated at the time of review.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Xylan reducing-end structure and/or RG-I-associated pectic domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper xylan deposition and affects RG-I branching
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. parvus/glz1/gatl1 mutants have reduced xylan and collapsed xylem despite normal/elevated xylan synthase activity. 2. The mutant also shows reduced RG-I branching, linking the gene to pectin-related wall structure. 3. GATL1 may synthesize a glycan substructure required for xylan deposition.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying secondary wall architecture, but high risk of vascular and reproductive defects
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Humidity-dependent dwarf phenotype and indehiscent anthers
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Enzymatic activity not demonstrated in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Elongation step; upstream/core polymer backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers D-GalA from UDP-D-GalA to the nonreducing end of homogalacturonan acceptors, elongating the HG backbone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major structural pectic polymer contributing to primary wall integrity, flexibility, adhesion, and load-bearing properties
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GAUT7; QUA3 (putative); KOR1 (putative); DRP2A/ADL6 (putative); DRP2B/ADL3 (putative); ribophorin I homolog (putative); ribophorin II homolog (putative)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed widely in most Arabidopsis tissues and developmental stages, including meristematic, vascular, reproductive tissues and tissues undergoing primary and secondary wall formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; core catalytic step for HG elongation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes homogalacturonan synthesis in pectin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical purification/proteomics; transient heterologous expression in HEK293 cells; immunoabsorption of HG:GalAT activity; coimmunoprecipitation with GAUT7
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAUT1 was conclusively identified as an HG:GalAT by recombinant expression and biochemical assays. 2. It elongates HG by adding GalA residues to the nonreducing end of HG acceptors. 3. GAUT1 functions as part of the GAUT1:GAUT7 complex in the Golgi.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering pectin amount and wall properties, but likely essential and difficult to manipulate without pleiotropic effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GAUT1 is posttranslationally cleaved and loses its transmembrane domain; requires GAUT7 for Golgi anchoring. No homozygous Arabidopsis mutants reported, suggesting essential function.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from UDP-Gal to elongate β-1,4-galactan side chains associated with RG-I
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms structural side-chain component of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to pectin structure and wall properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis; overexpression; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GALS1 was biochemically demonstrated to be a β-1,4-galactan synthase/GalT. 2. Loss of function reduces wall galactan, whereas overexpression increases it. 3. Recombinant GALS1 elongates β-1,4-galactopentaose using UDP-Gal.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to alter RG-I side-chain composition and cell wall material properties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Defined as one of the proven Arabidopsis pectin biosynthetic GTs in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose from UDP-Xyl onto L-Fuc in RG-II side chain A with α-1,3 linkage
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms structural motif of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential pectic domain for wall strength and plant growth; supports borate-dimer formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in seedlings and highly in vegetative tissues of mature plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in insect/Pichia systems; NMR; glycosidase digestion; mutant complementation-type substrate assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGXT1 is a proven RG-II α-1,3-xylosyltransferase. 2. It transfers Xyl onto L-Fuc in a structure unique to RG-II side chain A. 3. It contributes to RG-II assembly in the Golgi.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for engineering wall strength and fertility-related RG-II features, though likely with strong pleiotropic constraints
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: rgxt1 mutants reportedly lack obvious growth phenotypes individually.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose from UDP-Xyl onto L-Fuc in RG-II side chain A with α-1,3 linkage
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms structural motif of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports pectin wall architecture and RG-II structural integrity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in seedlings and highly in vegetative tissues of mature plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; NMR; enzyme digestion; mutant substrate assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGXT2 is a proven RG-II α-1,3-xylosyltransferase. 2. It modifies the L-Fuc-containing motif of RG-II side chain A. 3. It localizes to the Golgi and participates in RG-II biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for manipulating RG-II architecture, though utility may be limited by wall integrity constraints
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: rgxt2 mutants reportedly show no obvious growth phenotype individually.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose from UDP-Xyl onto L-Fuc in RG-II side chain A with α-1,3 linkage
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms structural motif of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed only in leaves and siliques in mature plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGXT3 is a proven RG-II α-1,3-xylosyltransferase. 2. It forms the α-1,3-Xyl linkage on the L-Fuc-containing RG-II side chain A motif. 3. Its expression is more restricted than that of RGXT1/2/4.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential for tissue-biased tuning of RG-II structure if functional redundancy can be managed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No rgxt3 mutant available at the time of review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose from UDP-Xyl onto L-Fuc in RG-II side chain A
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms structural motif of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports RG-II dimerization, wall strengthening, pollen tube and root growth
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Appears ubiquitously expressed and at higher level than other RGXTs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper RG-II side-chain xylosylation and normal RG-II dimerization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; localization; mutant phenotype and wall composition analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGXT4 is a proven RG-II α-1,3-xylosyltransferase. 2. Mutation reduces 2-O-methyl-D-Xyl in RG-II and decreases RG-II dimerization. 3. Loss of RGXT4 causes severe pollen tube/root defects, infertility, and seedling lethality.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important marker of RG-II-dependent wall strength but poor engineering target due to severe fitness costs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe fertility defects and seedling lethality when disrupted

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose from UDP-Xyl onto GalA residues in the HG backbone to form XGA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to specialized pectin structure in leaf walls
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant wall analysis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; microsomal transfer assay; product digestion with XGA hydrolase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. XGD1 is a proven XGA xylosyltransferase required for low-substituted XGA biosynthesis. 2. xgd1 mutants show reduced Xyl and loss of characteristic XGA oligosaccharides. 3. Recombinant XGD1 transfers Xyl onto endogenous and HG oligosaccharide acceptors.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May enable modification of specialized pectin fine structure with limited visible growth penalty
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact anomeric configuration of the reaction product remained to be fully established in the review; mutants showed no obvious growth phenotype.
